E-MTAB-14823: DNA Methylation profiles in girls with anorexia nervosa and amenorrhea: a pilot study
DNA Methylation profiles in girls with anorexia nervosa and amenorrhea: a pilot study

Using the Infinium Human DNA Methylation EPIC BeadChip array (850 K), we analyzed genome-wide DNA methylation patterns in peripheral blood leukocytes collected from 10 female patients with anorexia  with primary (n= 4) or secondary (n= 6) amenorrhea and 13 healthy pubertal girls matched by age (E-MTAB-13950).

column6: All blood samples were drawn at 8:00 a.m. from an antecubital vein, clotted, centrifuged, and serum was stored at −20 °C until analyses were performed.
Genomic DNA was extracted from peripheral blood leukocytes using standard procedures.
Bisulfite converted DNA (250 ng) was used for analysis of whole-genome methylation using the MethylationEPIC BeadChip (Illumina, San Diego, CA, USA).
column7: MethylationEPIC BeadChip (Illumina, San Diego, CA, USA)

column3: nucleic acid hybridization to array protocol
column6: Bisulfite converted DNA was whole-genome amplified for 20 h followed by end-point fragmentation. Fragmented DNA was precipitated, denatured and hybridized to the BeadChips for 20 h at 48 °C.
column7: MethylationEPIC BeadChip (Illumina, San Diego, CA, USA)

column3: array scanning and feature extraction protocol
column6: The BeadChips were washed and the hybridized primers were extended and labeled before scanning the BeadChips using the Illumina iScan system.
CpGs beta values were obtained using ChAMP on R (v. 4.2.0).
